name: Candied Grapefruit
recipeInstructions:
1. SCORE GRAPEFRUIT INTO QUARTERS and carefully pull off peels or juice fruits, cutting halves in half again.
2. Place peels in a large pan and cover with cold water; bring to boil.
3. Simmer for 25 minutes, then remove peels and let cool until easy to handle.
4. Gently scrape away most of the white pith just below the skin from cleaned peels.
5. Cut cleaned peels into strips using a knife or scissors.
6. In a 2-quart saucepan, mix sugar, water, and corn syrup; bring to boil.
7. Lower heat and cook until syrup is clear, then add peels and continue cooking slowly until translucent (about 1 hour).
8. Set rack over baking sheet and spread drained peels for about 15 minutes.
9. While peels drain, cover a large plate with superfine sugar.
10. Place several pieces at a time in the sugar and turn to coat; set aside on another plate or parchment paper coated with sugar.
11. Repeat until all peels are coated; let sit about 1 hour or until dried.
12. Package the sugared peels in a tin or plastic container and store in the cupboard or refrigerator.
13. To dip peels in chocolate, melt semisweet chocolate and butter over simmering water, stirring to ensure smoothness.
14. Dip each rind, coating most of it but leaving a small handle, or completely immerse.
15. Remove from chocolate with forks or chopsticks; set on parchment paper or rack.
16. Place in cool place until hardened; refrigerate stored pieces.
